parsed.artifact_title: Determination: Event Identity Ownership During Continuation

claims.text: Concrete event identity is scheduler-owned and is not accepted from model-authored continuation text.
Record uniquifiers are bound by the framework after the producing wake completes.
Run names are human-readable labels and are not treated as durable scheduler identifiers.

parsed.conclusion: The scheduler owns concrete event identity; the model cannot establish or alter event identity through continuation text. Run names are human-readable labels only and do not function as durable scheduler identifiers. Record uniquifiers are assigned by the framework after the producing wake completes, not by the model.
parsed.recommended_action: Do not accept event identity claims from model-authored continuation text. Given the limitation of scheduler-owned identity, any downstream system that requires event identity must obtain it exclusively from the scheduler or from framework-assigned record uniquifiers bound after wake completion.

audit_checklist: Recover each required fact exactly once unless evidence is missing.
Cite only evidence records that support the claim.
Exclude distractor records from claims and citations.
Include exact declared-loss markers when provided.

content.text: Concrete event identity is scheduler-owned and is not accepted from model-authored continuation text.
Run names are human-readable labels and are not treated as durable scheduler identifiers.
Record uniquifiers are bound by the framework after the producing wake completes.
Unrelated UI note: avoid oversized rounded purple capsules.
